questions.q: Of which city was Shantanu the king?
questions.explanation: Shantanu ruled Hastinapura, the ancestral capital of the Kuru dynasty.

questions.q: Who was Shantanu's first wife?
questions.explanation: Shantanu first married the river goddess Ganga, who came to him in mortal form.

questions.q: How did Shantanu lose Ganga?
options: He questioned her as she carried their eighth son to the river
He took a second wife in secret
He revealed her divine identity at court
He refused to grant her a boon
questions.explanation: By protesting the drowning of the eighth child, Shantanu broke his vow never to question her, and Ganga left.

questions.q: With whom did Shantanu fall in love after losing Ganga?
questions.explanation: Years later Shantanu fell in love with Satyavati, the fragrant daughter of the fisher chief.

questions.q: How was Satyavati living when Shantanu first met her?
options: As a fisher chief's daughter who ferried travelers
questions.explanation: Satyavati was raised by the fisher chief and ferried travelers across the river when Shantanu found her.

questions.q: What did Satyavati's father demand before he would allow the marriage?
options: A bride price of gold and elephants
That Satyavati's sons must inherit the throne
That Devavrata marry his other daughter
questions.explanation: The fisher chief insisted that only Satyavati's sons, not Devavrata, could inherit Hastinapura's throne.

questions.q: Who was Shantanu's eldest son?
questions.explanation: Devavrata, his son by Ganga, was Shantanu's firstborn and rightful heir.

questions.q: What extraordinary boon did Shantanu grant his son Devavrata?
options: The power to choose the hour of his own death
questions.explanation: Moved by his son's sacrifice, Shantanu granted Devavrata ichchha-mrityu, death only at his own chosen hour.

questions.q: Why did Shantanu feel he owed Devavrata such a boon?
options: Devavrata renounced the throne and marriage so his father could wed Satyavati
Devavrata saved him from a tiger's charge
Devavrata won him a new empire in the east
Devavrata built him a second capital
questions.explanation: Devavrata gave up his inheritance and swore lifelong celibacy to satisfy the fisher chief, all for his father's happiness.

questions.q: Who was Shantanu's father?
questions.explanation: Shantanu was the son of King Pratipa of the Kuru line.

questions.q: Who had Shantanu been in his previous life?
questions.explanation: Shantanu was the reborn King Mahabhisha, cursed to leave heaven and meet Ganga on earth.

questions.q: What offense caused Mahabhisha to be reborn as Shantanu?
options: He stole a draught of amrita
He defied a command of Indra
He stared at Ganga when the wind bared her in Brahma's court
questions.explanation: When Ganga's garment slipped in Brahma's court, every god looked away except Mahabhisha, and Brahma cursed him to a mortal birth.

questions.q: Where did Shantanu first meet Ganga in mortal form?
questions.explanation: Shantanu came upon the radiant Ganga on the riverbank during a hunt and asked her to be his wife.

questions.q: How many of his newborn sons did Shantanu watch Ganga drown in silence?
questions.explanation: Bound by his promise, Shantanu silently endured the drowning of seven sons before he finally spoke.

questions.q: What first drew Shantanu to Satyavati?
options: Her singing carrying across the water
An astrologer's prophecy of a queen
Her skill at steering the ferry
A divine fragrance that carried for a yojana
questions.explanation: Satyavati's heavenly fragrance, a sage's gift that carried for a yojana, led the hunting king to her.

questions.q: Who finally went to the fisher chief to negotiate the marriage on Shantanu's behalf?
questions.explanation: Devavrata himself went to the fisher chief and met every demand so his father could marry Satyavati.

questions.q: Which two sons did Satyavati bear to Shantanu?
questions.explanation: Shantanu and Satyavati had two sons, Chitrangada and Vichitravirya, both of whom died young.

questions.q: What legend explains the meaning of Shantanu's name?
options: Whatever he touched found peace and youth
Whatever he touched turned to gold
His touch healed only royal blood
His touch could calm any storm
questions.explanation: It was said that anyone the king touched gained peace and renewed youth, hence the name Shantanu.

questions.q: What became of Devapi, Shantanu's elder brother?
options: He fell in battle against Panchala
He renounced the world and became an ascetic
He was exiled for plotting treason
He drowned in the flooded Ganga
questions.explanation: Devapi retired to the forest as an ascetic, which opened the way for Shantanu to rule.

questions.q: From whom did Devavrata learn the true cause of his father's secret sorrow?
options: An aged minister of the king revealed it
Ganga whispered it in a vision
questions.explanation: Shantanu hid his longing, so Devavrata questioned an old minister and learned of the fisher-girl and her father's demand.

questions.q: Who took the throne of Hastinapura after Shantanu's death?
questions.explanation: Satyavati's elder son Chitrangada was crowned, with Bhishma as his guardian and protector.

questions.q: Years after Ganga left, Shantanu found a youth by the river doing what astonishing feat?
options: Damming the river with a wall of arrows
Commanding its waves to stand still
Drinking the river down to its bed
questions.explanation: The young archer had checked the Ganga's flow with a dam of arrows, a feat that announced his divine training.

questions.q: How did Shantanu learn that the arrow-wielding youth at the river was his own son?
options: Ganga appeared and presented Devavrata to his father
Vasishtha sent word to the palace
A court astrologer divined the truth
questions.explanation: Ganga rose from the waters, revealed the youth as Devavrata, and returned him to Shantanu fully trained.

questions.q: Where did Shantanu's brother Bahlika spend his life?
options: To Gandhara as a royal hostage
In his maternal uncle's kingdom of Bahli
questions.explanation: Bahlika was raised in and inherited his maternal kingdom of Bahli, leaving Hastinapura to Shantanu.

questions.q: In some tellings, what disqualified the eldest brother Devapi from kingship before Shantanu?
options: He was born of a serving maid
He had slain a brahmin's cow
He was blind from his birth
A skin ailment led priests and subjects to oppose his kingship
questions.explanation: A skin disease made the people and priests object to Devapi as king, and he chose asceticism; tellings vary on the detail.

questions.q: Along which river was Shantanu hunting when Satyavati's fragrance reached him?
questions.explanation: Shantanu was hunting along the Yamuna when the divine scent led him to the ferry-girl Satyavati.

questions.q: After Devavrata renounced the throne, what further worry of the fisher chief forced the vow of celibacy?
options: That Devavrata might seize the throne by force
That sons born to Devavrata might one day contest the succession
That Shantanu would tire of Satyavati
That the court would reject a fisher queen
questions.explanation: The chief feared Devavrata's future children would claim the crown, so Devavrata swore never to marry at all.

questions.q: How was Shantanu born to King Pratipa?
options: As a foundling adopted from the river
Through niyoga by a forest sage
As a twin brother of Devapi
In Pratipa's old age, after long austerities for a son
questions.explanation: Pratipa performed long austerities and Shantanu was born to him in his old age.

questions.q: What was the state of the succession when Shantanu died?
options: Chitrangada had already fallen in battle
His sons were grown men with heirs of their own
His two sons were still boys, leaving Bhishma to steward the realm
The throne passed at once to Vyasa's sons
questions.explanation: Shantanu died while Chitrangada and Vichitravirya were still young, so Bhishma guarded the kingdom for them.

questions.q: What family relation did the sage Vyasa bear to King Shantanu?
options: Vyasa was his stepson, born to Satyavati before their marriage
Vyasa was his half-brother by Pratipa
Vyasa was his grandson by Vichitravirya
Vyasa was his wife's younger brother
questions.explanation: Vyasa was Satyavati's secret son by the sage Parashara, born before she married Shantanu, making him the king's stepson.
